topic.name: Naval Warfare History
topic.description: The documents discuss various classes of naval ships and their roles in historical naval conflicts, primarily focusing on the Imperial Japanese Navy and Royal Navy.

1: Japanese battleship Satsuma was built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the first decade of the 20th century.
The ship saw no combat during World War I, although she led a squadron that occupied several German colonies.
2: HMS Cambrian was a C-class light cruiser built for the Royal Navy during World War I.
Assigned to the Grand Fleet, the ship played only a small role during the war.
3: HMS Glowworm was a G-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy in the mid-1930s.
Glowworm was heavily damaged by Admiral Hipper, but still attempted to torpedo the German ship.
4: The Zuihō class was a group of two aircraft carriers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy before World War II.
Zuihō participated in the Philippine Sea and Leyte Gulf battles in mid-1944.
5: Mikasa served as the flagship of Admiral Tōgō Heihachirō throughout the Russo-Japanese War of 1904 – 1905.
She is now fully restored as a museum ship and can be visited at Mikasa Park in Yokosuka.
6: Zuihō was originally laid down as the submarine support ship Takasaki, later converted into an aircraft carrier.
The ship participated in many operations during World War II.
7: Libia was a protected cruiser built in Italy in the 1900s.
Before Italy's entry into World War I, she was involved in the evacuation of Prince William from Durazzo.
8: The Russian battleship Sinop was built for the Imperial Russian Navy, named after the victory at the Battle of Sinop.
She was converted to a gunnery training ship in 1910.
9: The Prinz Adalbert class was a type of armored cruiser built for the Imperial German Navy in the early 1900s.
Both ships saw extensive service with the German Navy.
10: The H class was a series of battleship designs for Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine intended to fulfill the requirements of Plan Z.
Due to the outbreak of World War II, none of the ships were ever built.

topic.name: State Highways and Routes
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways and routes across the United States and Canada, detailing their history, connections, and geographical significance.

Document 1: Maryland Route 12 is a state highway on the Eastern Shore in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The route runs 30.57 mi (49.20 km) from the Virginia border south of Stockton.
Document 2: King's Highway 412 is a tolled controlled-access highway in the Canadian province of Ontario.
The route is approximately 10 km (6.2 mi) long, connecting Highway 401 with the eastern extension of Highway 407.
Document 3: U.S. Route 412 is a U.S. highway in the south-central portion of the United States, connecting Springer, New Mexico to Columbia, Tennessee.
A 504.11-mile section of the highway crosses the state of Oklahoma.
Document 4: The Bayshore Freeway is a part of U.S. Route 101 in the San Francisco Bay Area of the U.S. state of California.
It runs along the west shore of the San Francisco Bay, connecting San Jose with San Francisco.
Document 5: King's Highway 7A is a provincially maintained highway in the Canadian province of Ontario that serves as a bypass of Highway 7.
The highway begins in the community of Manchester and travels east through Port Perry.
Document 6: State Route 174 is a state highway in the U.S. state of California.
The two-lane 13-mile highway connects Interstate 80 in Colfax with SR 20/SR 49 in Grass Valley.
Document 7: M-104 is a state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan in the Western Michigan region of the state.
It runs from Ferrysburg to Nunica in Ottawa County.
Document 8: Delaware Route 2 is a 10.94-mile-long east-west highway located in northern New Castle County, Delaware.
It runs from DE 72 and DE 273 east of Newark east to DE 52 in Wilmington.
Document 9: State Route 161 is a 36.25-mile-long state highway serving Pierce and King counties in the U.S. state of Washington.
The highway begins at SR 7 southwest of Eatonville and travels north as Meridian Avenue to Puyallup.
Document 10: State Route 243, or the Banning-Idyllwild Panoramic Highway, is a 30-mile two-lane highway that runs from Banning, California to Idyllwild, California.
The highway is a connector between Interstate 10 and SR 74.

topic.name: Popular Music and Performances
topic.description: The documents discuss various popular songs, albums, and live performances by notable artists across different genres.

1: Causing a Commotion is a song by American singer Madonna
The song became a top-ten hit in the United States, Australia, Canada, Ireland, Italy, New Zealand, Sweden and the United Kingdom
2: Live at Wembley is a live album by American recording artist Beyoncé
The DVD features her concert at Wembley Arena in London
3: Lost! is a song by the British alternative rock band Coldplay
The song was released on 10 November 2008 as the fourth overall single from the album
4: Wanna Be Startin' Somethin' is a song by American recording artist Michael Jackson
The song is characterized by a complex rhythm arrangement and a distinctive horn arrangement
5: Good Enough is a song by American rock band Evanescence
The song was critically acclaimed, with many music critics praising the piano sound
6: The Concert for Bangladesh was the collective name for two benefit concerts organised by former Beatles lead guitarist George Harrison
The shows were organised to raise international awareness and fund relief efforts for refugees
7: '03 Bonnie & Clyde is a song recorded by American rapper Jay-Z featuring American R&B singer Beyoncé
The song was released as the album's lead single on October 10, 2002
8: Bidi Bidi Bom Bom is a song recorded by American Tejano singer, Selena
The single peaked at number one on the United States Billboard Hot Latin Songs chart
9: In the Dark is a song performed by American recording artist Dev
The song received generally positive reviews from music critics
10: Music of the Sun is the debut studio album by Barbadian singer Rihanna
The music incorporates Caribbean music such as dancehall and reggae

topic.name: Professional Wrestling Events
topic.description: The documents discuss various professional wrestling pay-per-view events and their significance within the wrestling industry.

Document 1: WrestleMania XI was the eleventh annual WrestleMania pay-per-view event held by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF)
The match between Taylor and Bigelow brought the WWF mainstream press coverage.
Document 3: ECW One Night Stand was a professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE)
The main event was a tag team match between The Dudley Boyz and the team of Tommy Dreamer and The Sandman.
Document 4: Taboo Tuesday was a professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE)
The fans were given the chance to vote on stipulations for the matches.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclone Events
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclones, their development, impacts, and meteorological characteristics across different regions and years.

Document 1: Hurricane Dolores was regarded as the worst hurricane to strike Acapulco since 1938.
Hurricane Dolores produced heavy rains that triggered widespread flooding and mudslides.
Document 2: The 2002 North Indian Ocean cyclone season was a below active season in terms of tropical cyclone formation.
Overall, there was a total of seven depressions and four cyclonic storms.
Document 4: The 1943 Surprise hurricane was the first hurricane to be entered by a reconnaissance aircraft.
The storm was considered the worst in Texas since the 1915 Galveston hurricane.
Document 5: Hurricane Fran was a long-tracked Category 1 hurricane that caused little damage during its existence.
The system was eventually classified as a subtropical depression on October 8.

the single species Caloscypha fulgens, commonly known as the spring orange peel fungus

8: Collybia tuberosa, commonly known as the lentil shanklet
the type species of the genus Collybia

found widely along coastlines in the Indo-Pacific

is a species of shark in the family Squatinidae

topic.name: Role-Playing Video Games
topic.description: The documents discuss various role-playing video games, their development, gameplay mechanics, and narratives.

Document 1: Final Fantasy III is a role-playing video game developed and published by Square in 1990
the first numbered Final Fantasy game to feature the job-change system.
Document 2: Sword of Aragon is a turn-based strategy and role-playing game developed and published by Strategic Simulations, Inc. in 1989
the protagonist embarks on a quest to unify the land through conquest.
Document 4: Tales of Vesperia is a Japanese role-playing video game developed by Namco Tales Studio
the story focuses on Yuri Lowell, a former Imperial soldier who forms a guild.

topic.name: Political Events and Figures
topic.description: This cluster discusses various political events and notable figures in American and Slovenian history, highlighting elections, political activism, and governance.

Document 1: The 2007 Slovenian presidential election was held in order to elect the successor to the second President of Slovenia Janez Drnovšek for a five-year term.
Türk won the second round in a landslide, with 68.03 % of the vote.
Document 2: American vice-presidential candidate Sarah Palin fell victim to a prank call by the Masked Avengers.
Both the McCain and Obama campaigns released light-hearted statements about the prank.
Document 3: General Joel Palmer was an American pioneer of the Oregon Territory in the Pacific Northwest region of North America.
He served as a member of the Indiana House of Representatives.
Document 4: Thomas Cushing III was an American lawyer, merchant, and statesman from Boston, Massachusetts.
Cushing represented Massachusetts in the First and Second Continental Congresses.
Document 5: Jane Fonda is an American actress, writer, political activist, former fashion model and fitness guru.
She is a two-time Academy Award winner and two time BAFTA Award winner.
Document 6: William Jason Fields represented Kentucky's Ninth District in the U.S. House of Representatives from 1911 to 1923.
Fields secured the backing of the powerful Jockey Club political alliance and won a landslide victory.
Document 7: Gary Johnson is an American businessman, politician and the Libertarian Party nominee for President of the United States in the 2016 election.
He served as the 29th Governor of New Mexico from 1995 to 2003.

the delegation included Augusto Ramos Soares and Mariana Diaz Ximenez
Document 2: Great Britain competed at the 2010 Winter Paralympics held in Vancouver, Canada
Kelly Gallagher became the first Northern Irish athlete to compete in the Winter Paralympics
Document 5: The Rio de Janeiro bid for the 2016 Summer Olympics and Paralympics was a successful bid
the Brazilian Olympic Committee chose Rio de Janeiro as its bidding city to host the 2016 Summer Olympics and Paralympics
Document 8: Jon Pollock was selected to train with the British wheelchair basketball squad in 1992
He has played at four Paralympic games

topic.name: Cultural and Historical Representations
topic.description: The documents discuss various cultural, historical, and fictional representations across different mediums and time periods.

1: The episode was developed into a teleplay by Maria and Andre Jacquemetton
The Ferengi first appeared in the Star Trek: The Next Generation episode 'The Last Outpost'.
2: George Ripley was an American social reformer, Unitarian minister, and journalist associated with Transcendentalism
He became one of the founding members of the Transcendental Club.
3: The Ilyushin Il-30 was a Soviet turbojet-powered tactical bomber
It was apparently canceled before the prototype made its first flight.
4: The British Library is the national library of the United Kingdom
It holds around 170 million items from many countries.
5: SMS Hela was an aviso of the German Imperial Navy prior to and during World War I
She was lightly armed for a light cruiser.
6: Thalaba the Destroyer is an 1801 epic poem composed by Robert Southey
The story describes how a group of sorcerers work to destroy the Hodeirah family.
7: The Ocean Village Marina is one of three marinas in the British Overseas Territory of Gibraltar
Ocean Village Marina and the adjacent Marina Bay together have 330 berths.
8: The 3rd Parachute Brigade was an airborne forces brigade raised by the British Army during the Second World War
The brigade first went into action on 5 June 1944 during Operation Tonga.
9: The Chrono series is a video game franchise developed and published by Square Enix
The music of Chrono Trigger was mainly composed by Yasunori Mitsuda.
10: Edward Grant Barrow was an American manager and front office executive in Major League Baseball
He is credited with building the Yankee dynasty.

topic.name: Roller Coaster History
topic.description: The documents discuss various roller coasters, their construction, features, and historical significance in amusement parks.

Document 1: Intimidator is a steel roller coaster built by Bolliger & Mabillard at Carowinds.
It is currently one of the tallest, fastest and longest roller coasters in the Southeast.
Document 3: Montu is an inverted roller coaster at Busch Gardens Tampa Bay in Tampa, Florida.
When the ride opened on May 16, 1996, it was the world 's tallest and fastest inverted roller coaster.
Document 4: Goliath is a steel Hyper Coaster located at the Six Flags Over Georgia amusement park.
Goliath was announced to the public on September 1, 2005 and opened on April 1, 2006.
Document 6: Goliath is a steel coaster roller coaster at La Ronde located in Montreal, Quebec, Canada.
Construction commenced in September 2005, and the roller coaster opened to the public on May 13, 2006.

topic.name: Motorsports Events and Achievements
topic.description: This cluster discusses notable motorsport events, focusing on races in Formula One and NASCAR, along with significant achievements of drivers.

Document 1: NASCAR Sprint Cup Series stock car race held on September 26, 2010
The race was won by Jimmie Johnson
Document 2: The 2015 Brazilian Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race
Nico Rosberg took pole position and won the race
Document 4: The 2015 Malaysian Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race
Vettel won the race, having started from second place
Document 8: The 2015 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race
Nico Rosberg secured his sixth consecutive pole position and won the race
Document 6: Gilles Villeneuve was a Canadian racing driver
Villeneuve spent six years in Grand Prix racing with Ferrari, winning six races

topic.name: Equine and Canine History
topic.description: The documents discuss notable equine and canine breeds, their origins, notable individuals involved with them, and significant events in their histories.

Document 1: Naborr was a gray Arabian stallion foaled in Russia at the Tersk Stud.
He went on to become a leading sire of champion Arabian horses in the United States and Canada.
Document 3: The Blue Picardy Spaniel is a breed of Spaniel originating in France.
It is especially good with children.
Document 6: Old Jock was a famous Fox Terrier during the late 19th and early 20th centuries.
He was involved in the early formation of the Jack Russell Terrier and the Dandie Dinmont Terrier breeds.
Document 7: The Tennessee Walking Horse National Celebration is the largest horse show for the Tennessee Walking Horse breed.
The Celebration spans 11 days and nights in late August and early September annually.

topic.name: Culinary Traditions and Practices
topic.description: The documents discuss various culinary traditions, cooking methods, and iconic food products from different cultures around the world.

1: Cottage garden is a distinct style that uses informal design, traditional materials, dense plantings, and a mixture of ornamental and edible plants.
Flowers common to early cottage gardens included traditional florists' flowers such as primroses and violets.
2: 'Pink slime' is a meat-based product used as a food additive to ground beef and beef-based processed meats.
The product is regulated in different manners in various regions.
4: The Whopper is the signature hamburger product sold by the international fast-food restaurant chain Burger King.
Burger King occasionally releases limited-time variants on the Whopper.
5: French cuisine consists of the cooking traditions and practices from France.
Knowledge of French cooking has contributed significantly to Western cuisines.
6: Deep frying is a cooking method in which food is submerged in hot fat, most commonly oil.
Deep frying is popular worldwide, with deep-fried foods accounting for a large portion of global caloric consumption.
7: Mediterranean cuisine is the food from the lands around the Mediterranean Sea and its preparation.
The cooking of the area is not to be confused with the Mediterranean diet.
8: Ya Kun Kaya Toast is a Singaporean chain of mass-market cafes selling toast products.
The stall gradually developed a reputation for delicious kaya toast and friendly service.
10: Fried chicken is a dish consisting of chicken pieces usually from broiler chickens which have been floured or battered and then pan-fried, deep fried, or pressure fried.
Crisp well-seasoned skin, rendered of excess fat, is a hallmark of well made fried chicken.

topic.name: Fantasy and Science Fiction Media
topic.description: This topic encompasses various forms of fantasy and science fiction literature and media, including pulp magazines, comics, and novels, highlighting their historical significance and cultural impact.

1: Strange Tales was a U.S. pulp magazine first published from 1931 to 1933 specializing in fantasy and weird fiction.
It was a significant competitor to Weird Tales, the leading magazine in the field.
2: Anne Rice is an American author of gothic fiction and is best known for her influential series of novels, The Vampire Chronicles.
Her works revolve around themes of fantasy and the supernatural.
3: Cobra is a Japanese manga series set in the far future, telling the story of an adventurous protagonist in a blend of genres.
It combines elements of science fiction with action and adventure.
4: Valérian and Laureline is a French science fiction comics series focusing on the adventures of a spatio-temporal agent and his female colleague.
The series mixes space opera with time travel plots.
5: Out of This World Adventures was a pulp magazine which published science fiction stories and comics in the early 1950s.
It reflects the dramatic changes in the world of U.S. science fiction publishing.
6: Space Science Fiction Magazine published short stories by well-known writers, including Arthur C. Clarke, in the late 1950s.
It was part of a package of genre magazines and radio shows.
7: The Terminator is a science fiction action-thriller film that combines elements of time travel and futuristic technology.
It helped launch a franchise that includes films, comics, and video games.

topic.name: Mathematical Concepts and Theorems
topic.description: This cluster discusses various mathematical concepts and theorems that are foundational in calculus, physics, and electrical engineering.

1: The derivative of a function of a real variable measures the sensitivity to change
Derivatives are a fundamental tool of calculus.
5: A Taylor series is a representation of a function as an infinite sum of terms
Taylor's theorem gives quantitative estimates on the error introduced by the use of such an approximation.
10: A Hilbert space generalizes the notion of Euclidean space
Hilbert spaces arise naturally and frequently in mathematics and physics.

topic.name: Bach's Sacred Music
topic.description: The documents focus on the sacred vocal compositions of Johann Sebastian Bach, including oratorios, cantatas, and masses, reflecting his contributions to liturgical music.

1: Lobet Gott in seinen Reichen (Laud to God in all his kingdoms), BWV 11, known as the Ascension Oratorio
structured in eleven movements in two parts, taking about half an hour to perform.
3: Gott ist mein König (God is my King), BWV 71, is a cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach written in Mühlhausen
scored festively with a Baroque instrumental ensemble including trumpets and timpani.
8: The Mass in B minor is Johann Sebastian Bach's only setting of the complete Latin text of the Ordinarium missae
Bach structured the work in four parts: No. 1 Missa No. 2 Symbolum Nicenum No. 3 Sanctus No. 4 Osanna, Benedictus, Agnus Dei et Dona nobis pacem.
10: Johann Sebastian Bach's Magnificat is a musical setting of the biblical canticle Magnificat
It is the first major liturgical composition on a Latin text by Bach.

topic.name: Legal and Judicial Systems
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of legal systems, including court structures, notable legal figures, and significant legal cases across different jurisdictions.

1: The United States District Court for the Eastern District of Missouri is a trial level federal district court based in St. Louis, Missouri.
Judges of this court preside over civil and criminal trials on federal matters that originate within the borders of its jurisdiction.
2: Peter Aquino Aduja was the first Filipino American elected to public office in the United States.
He worked for the Hawaiian Department of Attorney General, and served two years as a judge.
3: The legal issues of Burger King include several legal disputes and lawsuits, as both plaintiff and defendant.
These situations have touched on the concepts of animal rights, corporate responsibility and ethics, as well as social justice.
7: The Constitution of the Commonwealth of Virginia is the document that defines and limits the powers of the state government and the basic rights of the citizens.
Virginia was an early state to adopt its own Constitution on June 29, 1776.

topic.name: Historical Churches and Festivals
topic.description: The documents discuss various historical churches and cultural festivals, primarily focusing on their origins, architectural significance, and community roles.

Document 1: Sängerfest is a competition of Sängerbunds, or singer groups, with prizes for the best group or groups.
Sängerbunds are still active in Europe and in American communities with Germanic heritage.
Document 2: St Ceidio's Church is a rural 19th-century church near Llannerch-y-medd, in Anglesey, north Wales.
The present building is no longer used for services, but has been looked after by local people.
Document 4: Christ Church, Newton is a Christian house of worship located in Newton, New Jersey.
The current church building, erected in 1868 – 1869, is the second structure built at the site.
Document 7: St Tyfrydog's Church is a small medieval church, in Llandyfrydog, Anglesey, north Wales.
The church is still in use for worship, as part of the Church in Wales.
Document 10: St Mary's Church is a small medieval church in Anglesey, north Wales.
It is a Grade II * listed building, a national designation given to 'particularly important buildings of more than special interest'.

topic.name: Cultural and Historical Narratives
topic.description: This cluster explores various cultural, historical, and artistic narratives across different regions and mediums.

1: Mogadishu is the capital and most populous city of Somalia.

2: Wall Street: Money Never Sleeps is a 2010 American drama film directed by Oliver Stone.
The film revolves around the 2008 financial crisis.
3: Salesforce Marketing Cloud is a provider of digital marketing automation and analytics software.
It was founded in 2000 under the name ExactTarget.
4: The Garden of Words is a 2013 Japanese anime drama film directed by Makoto Shinkai.
The film focuses on Takao Akizuki, an aspiring 15-year-old shoemaker.
5: Magic Alex is the nickname given to Yanni Alexis Mardas by The Beatles.
Mardas was involved with the group between 1965 and 1969.
6: Water supply and sanitation in Colombia has been improved in many ways over the past decades.
Access to improved sanitation increased from 67% to 82%.
7: Beyoncé is the fifth studio album by American singer and songwriter Beyoncé.
Its dark, intimate subject material includes feminist themes of sex.
8: The Purple Piano Project is the premiere episode of the third season of the American musical television series Glee.
It features the start of a new school year at McKinley High.
9: Billboard publishes news, video, opinion, reviews, events and style.
It is known for music charts, including the Billboard Hot 100.
10: Steorn Ltd is a small, private technology development company based in Dublin, Ireland.
It announced in August 2006 it had developed a technology which provides free, clean, and constant energy.
